title: Palace of Versailles is built
subtitle: Palace in Versailles, France and location of the Museum of the History of France
fact: The Palace of Versailles is a former royal residence commissioned by King Louis XIV located in Versailles, about 18 kilometres (11 mi) west of the city centre of Paris, in the Yvelines department of Île-de-France region in France.

title: Louvre Museum opens
subtitle: Art and archeology museum in Paris, France
fact: The Louvre, or the Louvre Museum, is a national art museum in Paris, France.

title: United States Capitol is built
subtitle: Meeting place of the United States Congress
fact: The United States Capitol, often called the Capitol or the Capitol Building, is the seat of the United States Congress, the legislative branch of the federal government.

title: British Museum opens
subtitle: National museum in London, United Kingdom
fact: The British Museum is a public museum dedicated to human history, art and culture located in the Bloomsbury area of London.

title: Kensington Palace is built
subtitle: Royal residence set in Kensington Gardens, London, England, UK
fact: Kensington Palace is a royal residence situated within Kensington Gardens in the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea in London, England.

title: Blenheim Palace is built
subtitle: Country house near Woodstock, Oxfordshire, England, UK
fact: Blenheim Palace is a country house in Woodstock, Oxfordshire, England.

title: St James's Palace is completed
subtitle: Royal palace in London
fact: St James's Palace is the most senior royal palace in London, England.

title: Panthéon is completed
subtitle: Mausoleum in Paris for the most distinguished French people
fact: The Panthéon is a monument in the 5th arrondissement of Paris, France.

title: Sultan Ahmed Mosque is completed
subtitle: Historic mosque located in Istanbul, Turkey, also known as Blue Mosque
fact: The Sultan Ahmed Mosque, popularly known as the Blue Mosque, is an Ottoman-era historical imperial mosque located in Istanbul, Turkey.

title: Jama Masjid, Delhi is built
subtitle: Mosque in Delhi, India
fact: The Masjid-i-Jehan-Numa, commonly known as the Jama Masjid, in Old Delhi area of Delhi, is one of the largest mosques in India.

title: Winter Palace is completed
subtitle: Former imperial palace in Saint Petersburg, Russia
fact: The Winter Palace is a palace in Saint Petersburg that served as the official residence of the House of Romanov, previous emperors, from 1732 to 1917.

title: Hawa Mahal is completed
subtitle: Palace in Jaipur, Rajasthan, India
fact: The Hawa Mahal is a palace in the city of Jaipur, Rajasthan, India.

title: Vaux-le-Vicomte is completed
subtitle: Nicolas Fouquet's castle in Maincy
fact: The Château de Vaux-le-Vicomte or simply Vaux-le-Vicomte is a Baroque French château located in Maincy, near Melun, 55 km (34 mi) southeast of Paris in the Seine-et-Marne department of Île-de-France.

title: Schönbrunn Palace is built
subtitle: Palace in Vienna, Austria
fact: Schönbrunn Palace was the main summer residence of the Habsburg rulers, located in Hietzing, the 13th district of Vienna.

title: Apostolic Palace is built
subtitle: Official residence of the Pope in Vatican City
fact: The Apostolic Palace is the official residence of the Pope, the head of the Catholic Church, located in Vatican City.

title: Royal Palace of Madrid is built
subtitle: Official residence of the Spanish Royal Family
fact: The Royal Palace of Madrid is the official residence of the Spanish royal family and is used primarily for state ceremonies.

title: Amber Fort is built
subtitle: Fort and palace in Rajasthan, India
fact: Amer Fort or Amber Fort is a fort located in Amer, Rajasthan, India.

title: Uffizi Gallery is completed
subtitle: Palace and art museum in Florence, Italy
fact: The Uffizi Gallery is a prominent art museum adjacent to the Piazza della Signoria in the Historic Centre of Florence in the region of Tuscany, Italy.

title: Palace of Whitehall is built
subtitle: Former building in the City of Westminster, London
fact: The Palace of Whitehall – also spelled White Hall – at Westminster was the main residence of the English monarchs from 1530 until 1698, when most of its structures, with the notable exception of Inigo Jones's Banqueting House of 1622, were destroyed by fire.

title: Hermitage Museum opens
subtitle: Museum in Saint Petersburg, Russia
fact: The State Hermitage Museum is a museum of art and culture in Saint Petersburg, Russia, and holds the largest collection of paintings in the world.

title: Castle Howard is built

fact: Castle Howard is an English country house in Henderskelfe, North Yorkshire, 15 miles (24 km) north of York.

fact: The Charminar is a monument located in Hyderabad, Telangana, India.

fact: The Belvedere is a historic building complex in Vienna, Austria consisting of two Baroque palaces, the Orangery, and the Palace Stables.

fact: The Vatican Museums are the public museums of the Vatican City.

fact: The Badshahi Mosque is a Mughal-era imperial mosque located in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan.

fact: The Château de Chambord in Chambord, Centre-Val de Loire, France, is one of the most recognisable châteaux in the world because of its very distinctive French Renaissance architecture, which blends traditional French medieval forms with classical Renaissance structures.

fact: The Royal Pavilion and its surrounding gardens form a Grade I listed former royal residence in Brighton, England.

fact: Sanssouci is a historical building in Potsdam, near Berlin.

title: Pidhirtsi Castle is built
subtitle: 15Th Century Ukrainian castle-fortress
fact: The Pidhirtsi Castle is a residential castle-fortress located in the village of Pidhirtsi in Lviv Oblast (province) western Ukraine, located eighty kilometers east of Lviv.

title: Palazzo Pamphilj is completed
subtitle: Palace in Rome, Italy
fact: Palazzo Pamphilj, also spelled Palazzo Pamphili, is a palace facing onto the Piazza Navona in Rome, Italy.

title: Palais de la Légion d'Honneur is built
subtitle: Building in Paris, France
fact: The Palais de la Légion d'Honneur, also known as the Hôtel de Salm, is a historic building on the Left Bank of the River Seine in Paris, France.

title: Old Synagogue in Kraków is built
subtitle: Former synagogue in Kazimierz, now Judaic Museum in Kraków
fact: The Old Synagogue was a former Orthodox Jewish congregation and fortress synagogue, located at 24 Szeroka Street, in the Kazimierz district of Kraków, in the Lesser Poland Voivodeship of Poland.

title: Atik Valide Mosque is built
subtitle: Mosque in İstanbul, Turkey
fact: The Atik Valide Mosque is a 16th century Ottoman imperial mosque located on a hill above the large and densely populated district of Üsküdar, in Istanbul, Turkey.

title: Pillnitz Castle is built
subtitle: Château
fact: Pillnitz Palace is a restored Baroque castle at the eastern end of the city of Dresden in the German state of Saxony.

title: Church of St. Peter and St. Paul, Vilnius is completed
subtitle: Church
fact: The Church of St.

title: Castle of San Sebastián is built
subtitle: Cultural property in Cádiz, Spain
fact: The Castle of San Sebastián is a fortress located in Cádiz, Spain, at the end of La Caleta beach on a small island separated from the main city.

title: Primate's Palace is completed
subtitle: Palace in Bratislava, Slovakia
fact: The Primacial Palace is a neoclassical palace in the Old Town of Bratislava the capital of Slovakia.

title: Egmont Palace is built
subtitle: Building in Brussels, Belgium
fact: The Egmont Palace, also sometimes known as the Arenberg Palace, is a neoclassical palace in Brussels, Belgium.

title: Cathedral of Funchal is built
subtitle: Cathedral in Funchal, Madeira Island
fact: The Cathedral of Our Lady of the Assumption in Sé, Funchal, Madeira, Portugal, is the cathedral of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Funchal, which encompasses all of the Autonomous Region of Madeira.

title: Château de Maintenon is built
subtitle: Historical monument in France in Maintenon, in Eure-et-Loir, in France
fact: The Château de Maintenon is a château, developed from the original castle, situated in the commune of Maintenon in the Eure-et-Loir département of France.

title: Sorgenfri Palace is built
subtitle: Palace in Denmark
fact: Sorgenfri Palace is a royal residence of the Danish monarch, located in Lyngby-Taarbæk Municipality, on the east side of Lyngby Kongevej, in the northern suburbs of Copenhagen.

title: Summer Palace of Peter I is built
subtitle: Palace in St. Petersburg, Russia
fact: The Summer Palace of Peter the Great was built in Saint Petersburg between 1710 and 1714 in the northeast corner of the Summer Garden, located on an island formed by the Fontanka River, Moyka River, and the Swan Canal.
